claimBoundary: This spec defines how future runs are recorded and aggregated. It does not declare that any benchmark, adapter, or model run has cleared a proof gate.

aggregation.headlineRule: A positive headline requires every seed artifact, contamination clean, protected metrics non-regressing, and a 95% CI on the primary delta excluding zero. Otherwise report candidate-only, within-noise, blocked, or negative.
aggregation.judgeRule: Semantic or model-judged claims require at least two independent judge families and agreement reporting. Deterministic verifier claims must identify the verifier and its held-out scope.
aggregation.failureRule: Failures, backend errors, empty responses, prompt leaks, and manual-review gaps are aggregated beside successes and linked to the failure ledger before public wording changes.
